phase_name: Multi-workstream planning complete — at execution boundary

blockers.workaround: Run supabase-schema.sql then supabase_migration.sql in Supabase SQL Editor

decisions.decision: INTG-01 through INTG-04 are v1 scope, not deferred to v1.5/v2
decisions.rationale: User stated explicitly: 'must be built in this first product launch'

decisions.decision: Push notifications use native APNs/FCM via Supabase Edge Functions, NOT expo-notifications or EAS
decisions.rationale: User stated: 'I don't want to use Expo' for push

decisions.decision: Push payloads never carry raw health-log data
decisions.rationale: Hard product rule; enforced by assertNoHealthData() server-side guard

decisions.decision: Google OAuth tokens stored per users.id, not household-shared
appointments.notes never synced to external calendar services
Dark mode auto-activates at nighttime hours for both Mom and Partner
Family Mode triggered by More tab menu item 'Begin Family Mode'
decisions.rationale: User choice A from planning Q&A

decisions.decision: Night-shift swap stored in household_events table (not Zustand-only)
Checkpoint before postpartum content ships for review
Conflict resolution: last-write-wins for Google Calendar sync

security_constraints: Never add service-role key to client code
Never hardcode the October 2025 test due date
No NativeWind — StyleSheet + theme.ts tokens only
RLS is the security boundary; client-side filtering is additional defense only
next_action: 1) Update REQUIREMENTS.md: move INTG-01-04 out of Candidate v1.5/v2 into v1 scope. 2) Rewrite push-notifications 01-01-PLAN.md for native APNs/FCM — remove all expo-notifications/EAS references.
